content.title: Organic Chemistry: IUPAC Nomenclature
content.objective: Master IUPAC naming rules for simple organic compounds. Essential for 8-10 JAMB questions.

concepts.title: Functional Groups
concepts.explanation: Groups of atoms that determine compound properties. Each has specific naming suffix.

concepts.title: Carbon Chain Numbering
concepts.explanation: Always number from the end closest to the principal functional group.

steps.title: Identify the Functional Group
steps.result: This will be a carboxylic acid (suffix: -oic acid)

steps.title: Count the Total Carbon Chain
steps.instruction: Count ALL carbons in the longest chain containing the functional group

steps.title: Number from Functional Group
steps.instruction: Start numbering from the end NEAREST to the functional group
steps.example: CH3-CH2-COOH: COOH is position 1, CH3 is position 2
steps.result: No number needed here (functional group always at position 1)

steps.title: Add Substituents (if any)
steps.instruction: If there are branches/side chains, give them lowest numbers
steps.result: Position + substituent name + main chain name

steps.title: Combine the Name
steps.instruction: Prefix + carbon number + substituent + position + suffix
steps.result: FINAL NAME: Ethanoic acid (also called acetic acid)

mistakes.why: Functional group must get LOWEST number
mistakes.examFrequency: Very High - Asked almost every exam

mistakes.mistake: Forgetting to change vowel before consonant
mistakes.why: Remove 'e' at end of prefix when adding 'oic' or other suffixes

mistakes.correct: CH3-C(=O)-OH is carboxylic acid, not just ketone

mistakes.why: Each branch needs a position number
Double/triple bond also gets lowest number

practiceQuestionsSection.title: 5 Practice Questions (Difficulty: Easy → Hard)
practiceQuestionsSection.instructions: Try these yourself BEFORE looking at answers

questions.question: What is the IUPAC name of CH3-CH2-OH?
questions.explanation: 2 carbons (Eth-) + hydroxyl group (-OH) = Ethanol. Common name: alcohol.

questions.question: What is the name of CH3-CH2-CH2-CH3?
questions.explanation: 4 carbons with no functional group = But- + -ane = Butane. This is a simple alkane.

questions.question: Name this compound: CH3-CH(OH)-CH3
questions.explanation: 3 carbons (Prop-) with OH group. OH is at carbon 2 (numbering from nearest end). Answer: 2-Propanol (isopropanol).
questions.keyLearning: Number from functional group end, not from left

questions.question: What is the name of CH3-COOH?
questions.explanation: 2 carbons with carboxylic acid group (-COOH). Eth- + anoic acid = Ethanoic acid. Common name: acetic acid.

questions.question: Name: CH3-CH(CH3)-CH2-COOH
questions.explanation: Main chain = 3 carbons (Prop-) with COOH, so 'propanoic acid'. Methyl branch at position 2 (closest to COOH). Full name: 2-Methylpropanoic acid.
questions.keyLearning: Always count substituents from functional group

suffixes.title: Functional Group Suffixes
suffixes.data: Alkane=-ane, Alkene=-ene, Alkyne=-yne, Alcohol=-ol, Ketone=-one, Aldehyde=-al, Carboxylic Acid=-oic acid

priorities.title: Numbering Priority (1=Highest)
priorities.data: 1. Carboxylic Acid, 2. Aldehyde, 3. Ketone, 4. Alcohol, 5. Alkene, 6. Alkyne

examTipsSection.title: How to Ace JAMB Questions on This Topic

tips.action: Always draw out the molecule structure when given a name or vice versa
Before naming, circle the functional group
Ensure functional group gets LOWEST number
Don't make mistakes on basic counting
Meth, Eth, Prop, But, Pent, Hex, Hept, Oct (1-8 carbons)

videoSection.title: Video Explanation (Optional)
videoTopics: How to identify functional groups (2 min)
